MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT DOUGLAS BRAY's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS is off calendar per notice of settlement filed on November 13, 2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Oct-17-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: RENO F.R. FERNANDEZ III, ESQ., 415.362.0449, FOR DEFENDANT DOUGLAS BRAY; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT DOUGLAS BRAY's MOTION to set aside and vacate default is granted pursuant to CCP 473(d). Defendant Bray has not made a general appearance in this action. Failure to bring a motion to quash does not affect a nonresident defendant's right to challenge a default for lack of personal jurisdiction. The default can be challenged by a motion under CCP 473(d). (Strathvale Holdings v. E.B.H. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1241, 1250-1251) (motion to set aside for lack of jurisdiction was not affected by nonresident's failure to bring motion to quash.) (See also Rutter Group, Civil Procedure Before Trial, 3:165.6.) Plaintiff has not met its burden of showing that Defendant has sufficient minimum contacts with California so the assertion of jurisdiction does not violate "traditional notions of fair play and substantial justice." (Vons Companies, Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434, 444.) Therefore, the default entered in this case is void for lack of jurisdiction under CCP 473(d). Defendant's excusable neglect argument in the alternative does not amount to a general appearance because Defendant's motion, read in its entirety, does not recognize the authority of the court to proceed. (Hamilton v. Asbestos Corp., Ltd. (2000) 22 Cal.4th 1127, 1147.) The Court does not reach that argument in light of the ruling on CCP 473(d). Defendant's motion for sanctions under CCP 473(c)(1)(A) is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
